author: doll-agent
content:
Major restructure: M-slab parallelism (OpenBLAS-style) + per-thread B-pack.

Architecture change in ferray-linalg/src/gemm/outer.rs:
- Extracted gemm_seq_avx2_f64() — sequential 5-loop Goto, runs the full nest with no internal sync.
- gemm_avx2_f64() now splits M into n_workers slabs UPFRONT, each rounded up to MR. Each worker receives a non-overlapping (m_start, m_size) range and calls gemm_seq_avx2_f64 on its slab.
- Eliminates the per-pc-iter rayon join/barrier that the old IC-parallel-inside-PC structure imposed (#655 limitation).
- Added B_PACK_TLS (sibling of A_PACK_TLS from #656) so each worker reuses a 1 MB B-pack buffer across calls; its sequential Goto packs B once per (jc, pc) into its private buffer.
- Bumped NC=1024 → 512 so per-thread B-pack (KC*NC*8B = 1 MB) fits in P-core L2 (1.25-2 MB on 14700K).
- 3 new tests added: 512x512x512 parallel, 513x257x129 irregular slab boundaries, 512x512x512 with alpha=0.5 beta=0.7. All 240 ferray-linalg tests pass.

Performance (N=square, 100-iter median, 20 warmup, 14700K):
| N | before #657 | after #657 | gain |
| 256 | 65 | 65 | (parity) |
| 320 | 72 | 105 | +47% |
| 384 | 96 | 129 | +34% |
| 512 | 153 | 172 | +13% (203% of single-core peak) |

Tried HAND_TUNED_MIN_DIM=192 — regressed at N=192 (per-thread B-pack of 288 KB outweighs the small kernel work at that size). Reverted to 256, where parallel branch reliably wins.

Accuracy (statistical_equivalence.py): 47/47 PASS unchanged. matmul 10x10 max ULP=81, 100x100 max ULP=11264 (both within thresholds).

Remaining gap to NumPy MKL at N=512: 3.3x (172 vs 568 GFLOPs). MKL is using all 28 logical cores via NUMA-aware coordination — closing that requires affinity pinning and possibly accepting E-core throughput on Intel hybrid (currently we explicitly avoid them).
